name: Lincolnville

meta_title: Lincolnville Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in Lincolnville, SC? Roden Law handles car accidents, truck crashes, and personal injury claims. Free consultation. No fees unless we win your case.
roads: US-17A (Main Road) is the primary corridor through Lincolnville and carries heavy commuter traffic between Summerville and Charleston, with frequent rear-end collisions during peak hours near the town's commercial areas. SC-165 (Jedburg Road) intersects US-17A at the edge of town where inadequate turn lanes create dangerous merging conditions. The proximity of I-26 interchanges funnels high-speed traffic onto local roads that lack shoulders and sidewalks. Old Trolley Road approaching from Summerville sees increasing traffic from new residential developments, creating congestion at intersections not designed for current volume.

Trident Medical Center — 9330 Medical Plaza Dr, North Charleston, SC 29406 — (843) 797-7000

service_area: Serving Lincolnville, Summerville, Ladson, and surrounding Dorchester County communities.
content_hook:
Lincolnville is a historic town in Dorchester County with deep roots in South Carolina history, founded in 1867 by formerly enslaved people. Situated along US-17A between Summerville and the greater Charleston area, the community faces growing traffic challenges as development expands along the I-26 corridor.
Roden Law's North Charleston office represents Lincolnville residents injured in car accidents, truck collisions, and other personal injury incidents on the area's increasingly congested roadways. Our attorneys understand the local court system in Dorchester County and have experience handling cases that go through the Dorchester County Circuit Court.
Under South Carolina law, you have 3 years from the date of your injury to file a personal injury lawsuit (S.C. Code § 15-3-530). Don't wait to protect your rights — contact Roden Law for a free case evaluation today.

faqs.question: What should I do after a car accident in Lincolnville?
faqs.answer: Call 911, get medical attention, photograph the scene and vehicle damage, exchange insurance information with other drivers, and contact a personal injury attorney before giving statements to insurance companies. South Carolina law gives you 3 years to file a claim (S.C. Code § 15-3-530), but preserving evidence early strengthens your case.

faqs.question: Which court handles personal injury cases from Lincolnville?
faqs.answer: Personal injury lawsuits arising from incidents in Lincolnville are filed in Dorchester County Circuit Court. Roden Law's attorneys regularly handle cases in this court and are familiar with local procedures and filing requirements.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to hire Roden Law for an injury case in Lincolnville?
faqs.answer: Nothing upfront. Roden Law works on a contingency fee basis — you pay no attorney fees unless we recover compensation for your injuries. Your initial consultation is always free.

faqs.question: Can I recover damages if I was partially at fault for my accident in Lincolnville?
faqs.answer: Yes. South Carolina follows a modified comparative fault rule, which means you can recover compensation as long as you are less than 51% at fault. Your award is reduced by your percentage of fault.
